label: aryepiglottic part of oblique arytenoid muscle
costal part of respiratory diaphragm muscle
cumulus oophorus oocyte complex
note: possible_match (UBERON:0001306 is the 'cumulus oophorus', defined as the cluster of cells alone (no oocyte included). The 'cumulus oophorus oocyte complex' (COC) is the combined structure of cumulus cells plus the enclosed oocyte. These are related but distinct entities: the COC includes the oocyte whereas UBERON:0001306 does not. A new term may be warranted unless the scope of UBERON:0001306 is interpreted to include the oocyte.)

label: anterior vertebral muscle
note: possible_match (UBERON:0008453 has 'anterior vertebral muscle' as a related synonym but refers to a single muscle (rectus capitis anterior), not the prevertebral muscle group. The NTR refers to the group-level concept (prevertebral muscles). Treat as possible_match only; the group-level term is absent from UBERON.)

label: multilayer ovarian follicle
note: possible_match (UBERON:0000036 (secondary ovarian follicle, synonym: preantral follicle) is defined as 'a maturing ovarian follicle that has two or more layers of granulosa cells, up to the onset of antrum formation.' The multilayer ovarian follicle as defined by the NICHD Ovarian Nomenclature Workshop (PMID:39549739) is the most advanced preantral stage with multiple complete granulosa layers. These may describe the same structure, but the NICHD classification treats multilayer as a distinct named stage separate from the classical secondary follicle. Human curator review recommended.)

label: corona radiata
note: possible_match (UBERON:0004682 'corona radiata of neuraxis' carries 'corona radiata' as an exact synonym but is an entirely different structure — a white matter mass in the brain. The HRA term refers to the ovarian/embryological corona radiata (innermost granulosa cell layer of the cumulus oophorus). These are unrelated anatomical structures sharing a name. This is a no_match; included here only to flag that the bare label 'corona radiata' will collide with the existing UBERON synonym and may need a disambiguating label such as 'corona radiata of cumulus oophorus'.)
